steps: Grow Pichia pastoris cells on agar plate containing desired antibiotic ( non-shaking 30°C 72 hours )
Cultivation
Add growth medium (BMGY) to baffled flasks 3ml-10ml growth medium in 30mL-100mL flasks to ensure sufficient aeration
Inoculate growth medium with single positive colony
Incubate at 30°C at 225rpm overnight 24:00:00 30 °C
Gene expression
Transfer cultures to sterile 50mL falcon tubes
Centrifuge and decant supernatant 2000 x g, 5 min
Add 10mL of sterile BMMY to used baffled flasks. Swirl and transfer to corresponding falcon tube
Vortex falcon tube
Centrifuge falcon tube at 2000 x g, 5 min
Decant supernatant and add 10 mL of fresh BMMY
Vortext solution and transfer to original flasks
Incubate at 225 rpm overnight 24:00:00 30 °C
Continued expression
Add 50 µL sterile 100% methanol to flask
Incubate at 30°C at 225 rpm overnight 24:00:00 30 °C
Cell harvesting
Transfer cells to 50 mL falcon tubes from second day and harvest by centrifuging at 2000 x g, 5 min
Decant supernatant and resuspend cell wall containing pellet in 1 mL water.
Transfer to pre-weighed 2 mL tube
Centrifuge, decant water and use cells for desired experiments 10000 x g, 2 min

For each step where media was added, only 3 mL instead of 10 mL were added.All centrifugation steps were performed at 3.000 x g instead of 2.000 x g and only 20 μL of sterile 100 % methanol were added for methanol induction
Grow liquid cultures with starting volume of 3 mL in similar procedure From now on: use plastic tubes
Store harvested cells at -20°C
creator: Igem Dusseldorf
